dossiers.bear_case: Stock down ~24% pre-market to ~$19 despite EPS beat — market pricing in structural BPO decline as AI replaces labor; operating income -35.7% YoY signals accelerating margin compression; entering Monday would be buying into a free-fall
dossiers.bull_case: Deep value (P/B=0.55, dividend 5.6%); record FCF ($242M in Q2); iX Suite AI deal volume up 400% YoY could signal revenue inflection; insiders and smart money holders positioned
dossiers.catalyst: Q2 FY2026 earnings (Jun 29): EPS $2.63 slightly beat $2.57 est, but operating income collapsed -35.7% YoY, non-GAAP EPS -2.6% YoY; full-year guidance implies 0.25%-1.25% revenue growth at flat margins
dossiers.disqualify_reason: Guidance bomb — stock down ~24% pre-market ($25.23→$19.19) after Jun 29 earnings; operating income -35.7% YoY; structural BPO headwind confirmed; move is done
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Move is DONE and then some — the -24% pre-market gap is the market's verdict on guidance. Catalyst fully resolved (negatively). No upside thesis for a 5-day hold.

dossiers.bear_case: No earnings (P/E 5323) — burning cash. $1.35B in 7.125% junk bonds adds leverage in a higher-rate environment. Construction equipment rental is cyclical and rate-sensitive. Activist 13D thesis can take months to resolve, longer than the 5-day hold window.
dossiers.bull_case: Co-founder buys $2.87M at these prices — they know the business best. Fresh activist 13D often precedes M&A, sale, or strategic review announcement. $1.35B debt raise could finance a major acquisition or buyback. Stock is 43% below 52-week high ($35.50→$20.26) with no material negative news. Equipment rental digitization is a durable theme. 2.02x volume anomaly suggests informed accumulation over the past week.
dossiers.catalyst: Co-founders Jabbok & William Schlacks bought $2.87M; fresh Schedule 13D activist filing; $1.35B senior secured 2nd-lien note offering upsized Jun 16 (signals major strategic activity); volume 2.02x 30-day average
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Activist 13D resolution: 4-12 weeks; volume surge implies near-term catalyst within 1-2 weeks
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: NOT priced in. At $20.26 (43% below 52-week high), the market has not recognized the co-founder accumulation or the strategic activity signaled by the debt raise + activist 13D. Volume surge suggests early-stage informed buying.

dossiers.bear_case: P/E -35.74 (losing money), stock still 45% below 52-week high ($30.88). The 21.5% bounce from low may already capture the near-term catalyst — remaining upside is uncertain over 5 days.
dossiers.bull_case: Digital banking SaaS (Alkami Technology) with 1,225 employees and recurring revenue — activist targeting a beaten-down fintech at 52-week lows. Insider cluster buying alongside activist is high-conviction signal. 21.5% bounce from low suggests momentum is turning. Sector consolidation ongoing — M&A premium possible.
dossiers.catalyst: Activist 13D filing + insider cluster buying + 1.67x volume anomaly; stock bounced 21.5% off 52-week low set Jun 22 ($13.98→$17.00)
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Activist engagement: 4-12 weeks; recent 52-week low bounce is in early stages
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Partially priced in — the 52-week low bounce has begun (21.5% in 8 days). Activist catalyst and further M&A upside are not yet priced in.

dossiers.bear_case: Stock fell 79% from 52-week high ($53.13→$11.30) — short sellers have been right so far. Agricultural commodity input costs are volatile. Consumer discretionary spending is pressured. Insider buying without a specific near-term catalyst may just mean they think long-term value exists.
dossiers.bull_case: Profitable (P/E=10.88) pasture-raised egg/butter brand with 32.5% of float short. Any positive development — earnings beat, retail partnership, or just a market up-day — could trigger short covering cascade. Insiders buying at $11 know the business is stabilizing.
dossiers.catalyst: Insider cluster buying + short squeeze (32.5% short float); stock at multi-year lows after 79% decline from 52-week high
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Short squeeze can trigger within days given 32.5% float short; insider catalyst is medium-term (weeks)
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Not priced in — insiders are buying at multi-year lows with substantial short interest. Any positive signal could trigger covering.

dossiers.bear_case: P/B=58.6x (extreme), P/E=-2.26 (losing money), daily volume only 200K shares, 52-week high $94.06 (stock down 72%). Mortgage origination is unprofitable in the current rate environment. Thin trading means spreads are wide ($24.83 bid / $33.65 ask in pre-market). Going-concern risk cannot be ruled out.
dossiers.bull_case: 7.5M share float with 32.7% short — any buying pressure on this thin float could create a dramatic short squeeze. Better.com (digital mortgage) would be a direct beneficiary of any positive rate environment news. Insider buying into beaten-down stock at $26 vs peak $94.
dossiers.catalyst: Insider cluster buying + short squeeze (32.7% short float); micro-float (7.5M shares) creates outsized squeeze potential
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Short squeeze can trigger within 1-3 days on thin float
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: The squeeze thesis is not priced in. BUT the illiquidity is a double-edged sword — wide spreads can destroy entry/exit economics.

dossiers.bear_case: P/E=-100.71 (significant losses), P/B=54.62x (extremely rich on book value). Pharma compounding faces ongoing FDA regulatory uncertainty. No specific near-term catalyst identified in recent 8-K (Jun 22 was annual meeting).
dossiers.bull_case: Harrow (ImprimisRx) is a specialty pharma compounding company with 23.3% short float and insider accumulation. 52-week range $28.54-$54.85 — at $41.15 there's room to move to prior highs. Insiders buying signals positive developments in drug development pipeline or compounding business.
dossiers.catalyst: Insider cluster buying + short squeeze (23.3% short float); pharma compounding (ImprimisRx) with pharmaceutical drug development pipeline
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Short squeeze and insider catalyst: 1-3 weeks
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Not priced in — but no specific near-term catalyst identified. The squeeze thesis needs a spark that isn't visible yet.

dossiers.bear_case: Stock down 76% from 52-week high — short sellers may be correctly pricing in customer churn or competitive pressure. P/E=-9.20 (unprofitable). Annual meeting approved 2M new shares (dilution). Restaurant industry headwinds persist.
dossiers.bull_case: PAR Technology provides POS/cloud software to restaurant chains (QSR/fast casual). 26.5% short float with insider buying at $17 vs 52-week high $72.15. At P/B=0.84, the stock trades near book value despite being a SaaS business — deep value if growth thesis holds.
dossiers.catalyst: Insider cluster buying + short squeeze (26.5% short float); restaurant tech SaaS (POS systems) at 52-week lows
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Not priced in — the book value floor and insider buying suggest limited downside; short squeeze could provide 5-day pop.

dossiers.bear_case: Company founded September 2024, only 7 employees, management services business with almost no operating history. Extreme volatility ($31.50-$236.19 range) signals meme/speculative nature. P/B=37.85x. Very low daily volume (134K shares). The insider 'cluster' may be the founders themselves — not independent signal.
dossiers.bull_case: 4M share float with 24.1% short — extremely thin float means any buying creates outsized price moves. Arculus technology (digital asset cold storage in metal cards) is a trendy niche. Stock has already fallen from $236.19 (52-week high) to $138.87 — potential mean reversion if short sellers cover.
dossiers.catalyst: Insider cluster buying + short squeeze (24.1% short float); micro-float (4M shares) metal payment card / Arculus digital asset cold storage company
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Short squeeze on thin float: can trigger within 1-2 days
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Impossible to judge fairly — insufficient operating history. The squeeze thesis is the entire play.

dossiers.bear_case: Q1 FY27 showed EBITDA -24.8% to -$10.5M (worsening). New CFO as of June 15 — transition uncertainty. Refines FY27 outlook (likely lowered). Consumer durables are rate-sensitive. Low volume (280K/day) limits squeeze dynamics.
dossiers.bull_case: CEO Shawn Nelson has large personal stake and buying. 23.5% short float with major product innovation cycle ahead ('most prolific year of new product introductions'). New CFO signals operational reset. Stock recovered from March lows ($10.33) to $16.76.
dossiers.catalyst: Insider cluster buying + short squeeze (23.5% short float); Q1 FY27 results Jun 11 (flat revenue -0.1%, EBITDA -24.8%), new CFO appointed Jun 15, major product launches planned H2 FY27
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Short squeeze: days to weeks; product launch catalyst: H2 2026 (beyond 5-day hold)
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Q1 weakness is priced in; product launch upside is not yet in the stock. But near-term (5-day) catalysts are limited.

dossiers.bear_case: WITHDREW planned equity offering 'due to market conditions' (Jun 11) — institutional investors were not willing to subscribe at current prices. ATM offering is ongoing dilution. Biotech with no approved products and $11.1B market cap is speculative. Stock down 54% from 52-week high ($30.98).
dossiers.bull_case: Ivonescimab Phase III HARMONi-6 data released June 1 — if positive interpretation gains traction, stock could re-rate. Management (CEO Duggan, CFO Soni) buying shares signals confidence in data. 28.2% short float with stock at 52-week lows = outsized squeeze potential. Stock up 14% from June 10 lows already.
dossiers.catalyst: Insider cluster buying (ATM purchases, management indicated Jun 3) + short squeeze (28.2% short float); ivonescimab Phase II + Phase III data released Jun 1; stock bouncing from 52-week low ($12.55 Jun 10)
dossiers.catalyst_timing: Clinical data interpretation catalyst: already released (ongoing re-rating); short squeeze: days to weeks
dossiers.priced_in_judgment: Mixed — clinical data is out but market hasn't fully priced it. The withdrawn offering is a negative overhang. Short-term catalysts limited to squeeze + ongoing data interpretation.
